title: Multiple Myeloma
chief_complaint: I have severe back pain that started suddenly when I bent to pick up a newspaper. Now I can't walk. I've been tired, losing weight, and getting infections frequently. My doctor said I had 'high protein' in my blood last year but never followed up.

physical_exam.Back: Severe tenderness over T12 and L2 vertebrae. Unable to stand. Neurologic: Normal motor, sensory, reflexes in legs. No saddle anesthesia. No urinary retention.

hidden_data.spine_mri: Multiple vertebral compression fractures (T12, L1, L2). Diffuse marrow infiltration (low signal on T1, high on STIR). No epidural extension. No cord compression.
hidden_data.cmp: Total Protein 11.2, Albumin 2.8, Globulin 8.4, Creatinine 2.5 (AKI), Calcium 12.2 (hypercalcemia)
hidden_data.spep: M-protein spike (M-spike) in gamma region = 4.5 g/dL. IgG kappa monoclonal protein.
hidden_data.serum_free_lights: Kappa free light chain 850 mg/L (severely elevated). Lambda 10 mg/L. Kappa/lambda ratio 85 (normal 0.26-1.65).
hidden_data.urine: Bence Jones protein (kappa light chains) detected. 24h urine protein 3.5g.
hidden_data.bone_marrow: Hypercellular marrow with 60% atypical plasma cells (CD138+, CD38+). Normal hematopoiesis displaced. Amyloid deposits.
hidden_data.skeletal_survey: Multiple lytic lesions in skull, ribs, pelvis. Compression fractures T12, L1, L2.

correct_diagnosis: Multiple Myeloma (IgG Kappa) - Stage III (ISS) with Anemia, Hypercalcemia, Renal Failure, and Pathologic Vertebral Fractures (CRAB Criteria)
key_learning_points: Multiple myeloma = malignant plasma cell proliferation in bone marrow → monoclonal protein (M-protein) production → end-organ damage
CRAB criteria: Calcium elevated (>11), Renal failure (Cr >2), Anemia (Hb <10), Bone lesions (lytic on imaging). This patient has ALL FOUR (active myeloma requiring treatment)
Lab: M-spike on SPEP, monoclonal light chains (Bence Jones protein in urine), elevated serum free light chains with abnormal ratio. Bone marrow >10% clonal plasma cells

Skeletal survey or PET-CT for lytic lesions (XR can miss 20%). MRI for cord compression evaluation. Myeloma bone disease: ONLY lytic (not blastic). Alkaline phosphatase usually NORMAL (no osteoblast activity)

Treatment: NOT curable but treatable. Induction: Bortezomib + Lenalidomide + Dexamethasone (VRd). Autologous stem cell transplant if <70 and fit. Maintenance: Lenalidomide. Daratumumab (anti-CD38) for relapsed/refractory

Supportive: Bisphosphonates (Zoledronic acid) or Denosumab for bone disease. Hydration for hypercalcemia and renal protection. Avoid nephrotoxins (NSAIDs, contrast). Vaccinate (encapsulated organisms). Prophylactic acyclovir for herpes zoster
